food_subgroup: Lean pork, raw; tenderloin cut; lowest-fat pork cut, comparable to chicken breast in fat content

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw pork tenderloin as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage for raw meat cuts is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 100g raw portion of pork tenderloin, equivalent to a palm-sized steak before cooking; yields approximately 72g cooked after typical heat loss.

nutrient_source.title: Pork Tenderloin, Lean Only, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 168366)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy entry FDC 168366: Pork, fresh, loin, tenderloin, separable lean only, raw. All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: N/A — Pork Tenderloin, Raw: negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pork tenderloin raw contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g (USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and produces no measurable glycaemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign GI values to zero-carbohydrate foods. GL per serving = 0.0 by definition. glycemic_index set to -1 to indicate data not applicable rather than a measured value of zero.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (55mg/100g raw) and lean fat profile with minimal saturated fat (1.6g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Pork tenderloin raw contains only 55mg sodium per 100g — well below the threshold of concern for hypertension — and its low saturated fat content (1.6g/100g) avoids the arterial stiffness risk associated with high saturated fat intake, making it a suitable lean protein choice for individuals managing blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate (GI not applicable), high protein-to-calorie ratio, zinc (2.4mg) and magnesium (27mg) support insulin receptor signalling
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0g carbohydrate and a glycemic index that is not applicable, pork tenderloin raw causes no postprandial glucose rise; its high protein content (21.8g/100g) promotes satiety, and the zinc (2.4mg) and magnesium (27mg) content supports insulin receptor function, making it an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.6g/100g), MUFA-dominant fat profile, high selenium (37.4mcg) with antioxidant properties, and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pork tenderloin raw has a favourable cardiovascular fat profile — only 1.6g saturated fat per 100g with monounsaturated fat predominating — combined with high selenium (37.4mcg, ~68% RDA) which supports antioxidant defence, and low sodium (55mg), making it a heart-appropriate lean protein when prepared without added salt or saturated fats.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat, high selenium (37.4mcg antioxidant), high thiamine B1 (0.87mg supports energy metabolism)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The very low saturated fat content (1.6g/100g), high selenium (37.4mcg = ~68% RDA, supporting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory pathways), and exceptional thiamine B1 (0.87mg = ~73% RDA, supporting energy metabolism relevant to RA fatigue) make pork tenderloin raw a suitable lean protein for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Tenderloin (Raw) is the single leanest cut of pork, sourced from the psoas major muscle running along the spine, and is available raw at Singapore supermarkets including Cold Storage, Marketplace, and Don Don Donki. Per 100g raw: 143 kcal, 21.8g protein, 4.6g total fat (only 1.6g saturated — comparable to skinless chicken breast), 0g carbohydrate, 55mg sodium. The fat profile is MUFA-dominant (approximately 1.93g monounsaturated fat), making it cardiovascularly favourable. Nutritional highlights include exceptional thiamine B1 (0.87mg = ~73% RDA), high selenium (37.4mcg = ~68% RDA), potassium (424mg), phosphorus (247mg), and zinc (2.4mg). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 72g cooked after typical moisture loss. The common stereotype that pork is fatty does not apply to the tenderloin cut — it is nutritionally comparable to chicken breast in fat content. Note: pork is not halal. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366. Confidence: high.
common_user_queries: pork tenderloin vs chicken breast nutrition
how much fat in pork tenderloin

confidence_reason: All nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366 (Pork, fresh, loin, tenderloin, separable lean only, raw) — an exact match to the food described. This is a primary approved source with full nutrient panel coverage. No estimation or analogy was required for core macros or secondary nutrients.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as pork tenderloin is a pure muscle meat with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366. Sugar_g and dietary_fibre_g are naturally zero for the same reason. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366. Folate_mcg_dfe is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g per 100g); glycemic_index field set to -1 to indicate the value is not applicable rather than a measured zero — the University of Sydney GI Database does not assign GI values to zero-carbohydrate foods. Monounsaturated_fat_g (1.93g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.52g) are sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366 fatty acid breakdown. Water_g (73.0g) is sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 168366. Note: pork is not halal and is not suitable for Muslim consumers. This record is a generic raw ingredient reference — not a prepared dish. Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 72g cooked. Available at Cold Storage, Marketplace, and Don Don Donki in Singapore.
